creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
xfreerdp and remmina-plugin-rdp both crash on startup with an illegal instruction error.

Workaround: Build the port from source with -DWITH_DEBUG.

I'm guessing the optimizer is being too optimistic about CPU features for my old AMD processor.

xfreerdp startup output:

[15:07:41:916] [75443:08806400] [INFO][com.freerdp.utils] - Registering signal hook...
[15:07:41:925] [75443:08806400] [WARN][com.freerdp.client.common.cmdline] - Using deprecated command-line interface!
[15:07:41:925] [75443:08806400] [WARN][com.freerdp.client.common.compatibility] - mortimer.hpc.uwm.edu -> /v:mortimer.hpc.uwm.edu
[15:07:41:925] [75443:08806400] [WARN][com.freerdp.client.common.compatibility] - 
[15:07:41:926] [75443:08807400] [INFO][com.freerdp.client.x11] - No user name set. - Using login name: bacon
Illegal instruction (core dumped)

uname:

FreeBSD outpaddling.cs  bacon ~ 415: uname -a
FreeBSD outpaddling.cs.uwm.edu 10.3-RELEASE-p18 FreeBSD 10.3-RELEASE-p18 #0: Tue Apr 11 10:31:00 UTC 2017     root@amd64-builder.daemonology.net:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/GENERIC  amd64
